steps.title: Map the relationship between the two sentences
steps.content: Sentence before blank: Coleridge-Taylor emphasized his mixed-race ancestry by calling himself Anglo-African. Sentence after blank: he incorporated traditional African music into his compositions. Both describe him actively foregrounding his African heritage—the second is another example of the same behavior.

steps.title: Identify the transition type needed
steps.content: Two examples of the same pattern (emphasizing African heritage verbally, then musically) call for an additive transition that signals "here is another instance."

steps.title: Eliminate by rule violated
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘In addition,’: additive transition that correctly signals another example of the same behavior. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Actually,’: signals correction or contradiction of a prior claim; nothing is being corrected. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘However,’: signals contrast, but the two sentences agree rather than contrast. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Regardless,’: signals that what follows occurs despite the prior point; the second sentence reinforces, not defies, the first. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
